_meta.derived_from: Phase 2c Step 6 — pain-based refinement on the 16-review stratified corpus (3 on-site + 13 forum quotes) and 33 usable Meta ads.
_meta.small_N_flag: Corpus is 16 reviews. Pain clusters are based on the 4 locked personas + ad-library cross-reference. Cluster frequencies are directional only.

clusters.pain_label: Energy and sleep have collapsed in my 30s/40s
clusters.trigger: Constant fatigue, unrefreshing sleep, brain fog, weight gain despite training
clusters.desire: Feel like myself again — sharp, rested, motivated
clusters.representative_quote: I'm sleeping better, training harder, and my labs back this up. The oral protocol made it effortless.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Symptom-only — not yet on TRT, not a vet. The entry-edge persona.

clusters.pain_label: Tired of being tired — finally trying something
clusters.trigger: New prescription in hand, hoping it works, watching the clock for first signs of improvement
clusters.desire: Validation that this is the right path; visible improvement in weeks 1-2
clusters.representative_quote: My energy levels have improved, for sure. I'm hopeful.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Earlier-stage than the established user — measuring hope, not outcomes.

clusters.pain_label: I don't know what's wrong yet — show me the labs
clusters.trigger: Symptoms that won't resolve with lifestyle; standard primary care didn't help
clusters.desire: Comprehensive lab work that explains the symptoms and points to a plan
clusters.representative_quote: The diagnostics panel showed me exactly what was off. Two months later, totally different person.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Lab-first subset of Symptom — doesn't trust guesswork, wants data-driven entry.

clusters.pain_label: Years of weekly needles, traveling with syringes, the spouse/kids noticing
clusters.trigger: Plateaued results OR cumulative fatigue from the routine itself
clusters.desire: Equivalent or better results from a daily oral — no needles, no clinic
clusters.representative_quote: Long term test user, about 20 years. Tired of pinning but doesn't want a whole new set of problems.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Convenience-driven exit, NOT safety-driven. The 'I'm bored of the routine' veteran.

clusters.pain_label: Injections feel un-physiologic — peaks and valleys, all-day supraphysiologic levels
clusters.trigger: Learning about diurnal T rhythm and wanting a treatment that matches it
clusters.desire: A protocol that respects natural circadian T pattern (high AM, low PM)
clusters.representative_quote: The product follows a natural diurnal pattern — high morning peak, very low by night — which reduces HPTA suppression and aromatization.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Physiology-driven exit, not convenience-driven. The 'injections are un-biological' vet.

clusters.pain_label: Injections are pushing my hematocrit to dangerous levels
clusters.trigger: Lab showing HCT 50+; phlebotomy prescription; fear of stroke/clot
clusters.desire: A TRT that doesn't thicken blood — no phlebotomy, no extra lab worry
clusters.representative_quote: HCT rose from 48 to 56 in 2–4 months on a low dose. Looking for short-half-life options to minimize sides.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Medical-safety-driven exit, not convenience-driven. The 'blood-work-watching' vet.

clusters.pain_label: The brand looks like a Temu for testosterone
clusters.trigger: Mystery discount popups, AI-generated ads, 404s, half-finished site
clusters.desire: A telehealth clinic that looks like a real medical practice, not a dropship
clusters.representative_quote: 2 seconds on the page and I unlock a 'mystery discount'. What is this, Temu for roids?
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Surface-presentation guard, not science-presentation guard. The 'this looks shady' critic.

clusters.pain_label: The founder's background doesn't inspire medical confidence
clusters.trigger: Dr. Jaquish's online-degree background; his X3 Bar / weightlifting / 'cardio is useless' claims
clusters.desire: A medical-grade brand, not a wellness-influencer-founded one
clusters.representative_quote: Dr. Jaquish – Degree from an online mill; claims cardio and weightlifting are 'useless', sells rubber bands instead.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Founder-credibility guard. The 'who is behind this matters' critic.

clusters.pain_label: There are no published studies on this product
clusters.trigger: Looking for PubMed/white papers, finding only marketing claims
clusters.desire: Peer-reviewed or third-party data on Nandrogen's safety and pharmacokinetics
clusters.representative_quote: They claim DHT remains physiologic, which is laughable and impossible with a high dose of oral T. They also claim injections stop working after a couple months, as SHBG becomes elevated as 'a defense mechanism' – also completely false nonsense.
clusters.distinct_from_existing: Evidence-base guard. The 'where's the peer-reviewed PK data' critic.
